no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what needs to be built (new endpoint, model, deprecation) but does not specify exact code changes, line numbers, or implementation details like specific function bodies.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact function names (get_current_user_git_info), exact module paths (openhands.app_server.user.user_router), exact class names (UserGitInfo in openhands.integrations.service_types), and exact method names (get_user_git_info) across specific classes (UserContext, SpecifyUserContext, AuthUserContext, UserAuth). tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute actual Python code via subprocess, import modules, instantiate classes, call async functions, check return types, and verify runtime behavior (e.g., 401 response when not authenticated). They are not simple text-matching tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests verify non-trivial behavior: UserGitInfo model instantiation with field validation, optional fields defaulting to None, User alias being identical to UserGitInfo, abstract method checking, 401 exception raising with specific message. A stub returning None/empty would fail most of these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors mentioned in instruction.md are tested: new endpoint returning UserGitInfo, 401 on unauthenticated access, Pydantic model with required/optional fields, User alias, old endpoint deprecation flag, user context method integration.
